rationale: This reaction exemplifies the Takai-Utimoto Olefination, showing the conversion of an aldehyde (2-methylbutanal) to an (E)-alkenyl iodide using iodoform (CHI3) and chromium(II) chloride. The reactants are simple, acyclic, and clearly demonstrate the core transformation: R-CHO -> R-CH=CHX. The (E)-stereochemistry of the product is the typical outcome for this reaction. The use of iodoform is a classic variant.
collision_avoidance_note: This example uses simple aliphatic substrates to avoid overlap with reactions involving aromatic aldehydes (e.g., Perkin, Claisen-Schmidt) or complex ring systems. It specifically forms an alkenyl halide, distinguishing it from Wittig-type reactions that form C-C double bonds without a halogen. The key reagents (CrCl2, haloform) are characteristic of this transformation.
